supporting_agencies: ARC; CMS; DCEO; DHS; DOI; IESMA
status: IEMA-OHS works with partner agencies on awareness and installation projects throughout the state.
action: Promote the installation of smoke detectors and carbon monoxide detectors in residences throughout Illinois.

status: IEMA-OHS partners with FEMA to hold safe room funding workshops. IEMA-OHS funded a safe room project at Illinois State University.
action: Organize wind-resistant construction and/or safe room workshops.

status: New action item
action: Support the construction of tornado safe rooms in local communities' public buildings, public schools, and eligible private nonprofit facilities to FEMA standards.

supporting_agencies: Illinois State VOAD; University of Illinois Extension; American Red Cross
status: New action item
action: Support family preparedness programs throughout the state through existing community education sources.

status: New action item
action: Track local community hazard mitigation plans to ensure completion of new plans and updates to existing plans as their five-year cycle expires.

status: New action item
action: Use IEMA-OHS staff to encourage and track policies, capabilities, and implementation of actions in local mitigation plans.

status: New action item
action: Pursue mitigation of state owned/operated facilities.

status: New action item
action: Support community flood mitigation projects through federal grant support and local technical assistance.

status: New action item. State Water Plan.
action: Explore advances in technology and utilize data from new sources including LiDAR to generate preliminary-level 2-D models for the entire state for planning, response, recovery, and prioritization.

status: New action item
action: Assist communities with enrollment in FEMA's Direct Technical Assistance program.

status: Ongoing. IEMA-OHS is working with CMS and IDOT on this initiative.
action: Explore possibilities for the creation of a mobile support team and legislation to provide technical engineering and architectural staff capable of assessing structural safety of facilities for disaster resilience.

status: IEMA-OHS Public Sections provides training related to damage assessments. IEMA-OHS has created a training section to conduct on-site training for local jurisdictions.
action: Provide instructional and training opportunities for local code officials to conduct pre-disaster assessments of structural safety of facilities for disaster resilience.

status: New action item
action: Help local and State officials better understand Agricultural Disaster Declarations versus Presidential Disaster Declarations.

status: New action item
action: Develop more situational awareness between IEMA-OHS and Farm Services Agency for better response.

status: IEMA-OHS will update the IMAG team and share information with local agencies through annual maintenance of the State Hazard Mitigation Plan.
action: Send updated information on the PDM initiative to eligible municipal and county managers, local planners, and floodplain administrators annually.

status: Presented on IEMA-OHS monthly calls with Illinois counties.
action: Publicize Section 322 of the Disaster Mitigation Act of 2000 to local public officials in all outreach activities.

status: IEMA-OHS holds monthly meetings to discuss safety issues associated with gas and electric utilities.
action: Continue to educate the public about safety issues related to natural hazards at electric and natural gas utilities.

status: IEMA-OHS responds to media requests and promotes mitigation success through the IEMA-OHS website and regular media releases.
action: Provide information to media reporting on past disasters and mitigation in the aftermath of a disaster.

status: IAFSM and IEMA-OHS will continue to offer workshops throughout the state.
action: Work with IAFSM to conduct flood proofing workshops.

status: IEMA-OHS will continue outreach through annual training summit, regional conversations, and simulations.
action: Using the Earthquake Coordinator, conduct outreach and education on the potential impact of earthquakes on Illinois businesses and residents.

status: IEMA-OHS will provide information and materials related to hazard resistant construction methods to ISBE. New efforts include green infrastructure.
action: Work with ISBE to provide hazard resistant construction information and workshops to vocational schoolteachers.

status: Continue and expand training efforts across the state, including new building codes and green technology.
action: Provide workshops for construction workers on hazard resistant construction with construction unions and building associations.

status: Ongoing post-disaster outreach procedure action.
action: Develop and maintain post-disaster outreach procedures and assign staff to mitigation and/or recovery teams.
